mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 7.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving equity earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 7.0 percent over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion, which increases corporate revenue visibility and compresses equity risk premiums, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, indicating the combined indicators do not predict equity returns better than a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production peaking and flattening signals supply-side capacity constraints and demand exhaustion, which historically precedes earnings downgrades and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of observations where INDPRO growth decelerates for two consecutive months, falsifying the supply-exhaustion premise.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5 percent while GDPC1 growth decelerates below 2.0 percent, stagflationary pressures emerge, forcing the Federal Reserve to tighten policy prematurely and depressing equity valuations over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5 percent and GDPC1 growth decelerates below 2.0 percent
failure_condition: Equity returns do not decline significantly when the dual threshold is breached, or the signal generates a positive Rank IC over a 4-year backtest, indicating markets price in accommodative policy responses.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential investment and consumer credit demand, proxied by RSAFS, directly boost aggregate demand and corporate sales expectations, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows no statistically significant predictive power (p > 0.10) for 5-day returns when RSAFS trends upward, rejecting the demand-channel transmission mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between payroll growth and unemployment rate decline indicates structural labor market healing rather than cyclical noise, which sustains consumer spending power and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample prediction, or forward returns turn negative when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k monthly for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25 percent alongside accelerating CPIAUCSL, the real policy rate turns deeply negative, forcing equity investors to rotate into nominal assets and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Markets price in immediate inflation expectations without equity outperformance, resulting in a negative or flat Rank IC for the combined signal over a 3-year rolling window.
